data.description:
# `rebar create` accepts a file path for the ticket description (contents inlined)

## Context

Today `rebar create <type> <title>` takes the description as an inline string via
`--description "<text>"` (CLI), `description=` (library `rebar.create_ticket`), and the
`create_ticket` MCP tool. Authoring a rich, gate-passing description (the template requires an
`## Acceptance Criteria` block plus per-type headings, ≥~200 chars, bullets) as a single shell-quoted
string is painful and error-prone: newlines, backticks, `$`, and quotes must be escaped, and large
plans are unwieldy on the command line. Agents already work around this by writing the body to a temp
file and `cat`-ing it into `--description "$(cat file)"`, which is exactly the friction this removes.

This epic adds a way to pass a **file path** whose **contents become the description**. Critically,
the file is read at create time and its **contents are stored as the description** — the ticket does
NOT store a reference/path to the file (the file can be deleted afterward with no effect on the
ticket). This mirrors how tools like `git commit -F <file>` and `gh pr create --body-file <file>`
behave.

## What / Scope

In scope:
- **CLI:** add `--description-file <path>` (and a short alias if consistent with existing flags) to
  `rebar create`. Reads the file (UTF-8), uses its contents as the description. `-` means read from
  **stdin** (consistent with `gh`/`git -F -`). Mutually exclusive with `--description` (error if both
  given). Path errors (missing/unreadable/not a file) produce a clear non-zero exit with a useful
  message, not a traceback.
- **Library:** `rebar.create_ticket(...)` gains a `description_file: str | os.PathLike | None`
  parameter (or accepts the existing `description` plus a new `description_path`); reading is done in
  the library so all surfaces share one implementation. Inline `description` and the file param are
  mutually exclusive.
- **MCP:** `create_ticket` tool gains an optional `description_file` argument with the same
  read-and-inline semantics and mutual-exclusion rule. (Consider security: the MCP server may run with
  a constrained CWD — document/validate that the path is resolved relative to the repo root or an
  explicitly allowed base, and reject path traversal, consistent with the LLM file-tool containment
  already used elsewhere.)
- Docs: README CLI block + `rebar create --help` + CLAUDE.md tool notes updated to describe the flag
  and the "contents are inlined, not referenced" semantics.
- Tests: CLI (file, stdin `-`, missing file, both-flags-error), library, and MCP paths.

Out of scope:
- Templating/interpolation of the file contents (store verbatim).
- A `--description-file` for `edit` (could be a fast-follow; note it but don't build here unless cheap
  to share the same helper).
- Any change to how descriptions are stored/rendered.

## Design notes

- Put the read-and-validate logic in ONE place (library `create_ticket` or a small shared helper) so
  CLI/MCP both delegate — do not reimplement file reading per surface (this repo already has a
  cross-session-duplication problem; don't add to it).
- Decide the precedence/exclusivity rule explicitly and test it: passing both `--description` and
  `--description-file` is a usage error (exit non-zero), not a silent winner.
- `-` (stdin) is valuable for agents/pipes; implement it unless it conflicts with existing stdin use
  on `create`.
- Preserve the file contents exactly (including trailing newline handling decision — document it);
  the gate checks (`clarity_check`/`check_ac`) run on the stored description unchanged.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `rebar create <type> <title> --description-file <path>` creates a ticket whose stored
      description equals the file's contents; deleting the file afterward does not change the ticket
      (contents are inlined, not referenced).
- [ ] `--description-file -` reads the description from stdin.
- [ ] Passing both `--description` and `--description-file` exits non-zero with a clear error; a
      missing/unreadable path exits non-zero with a clear message (no traceback).
- [ ] `rebar.create_ticket(...)` exposes the file-path parameter with identical inline semantics and
      mutual-exclusion, and the CLI delegates to it (single implementation).
- [ ] The MCP `create_ticket` tool accepts the file-path argument with the same semantics and a
      documented path-safety rule (no traversal outside the allowed base).
- [ ] Docs (README CLI block, `create --help`, CLAUDE.md) describe the flag and the inlined-not-
      referenced behavior; tests cover file / stdin / missing-file / both-flags-error across CLI,
      library, and MCP.
- [ ] `make lint` + full test suite pass.

## Success Criteria

- Agents and users can author multi-line, gate-passing descriptions from a file (or stdin) without
  shell-quoting gymnastics, with one shared implementation across CLI/library/MCP and clear error
  behavior on misuse.
data.title: rebar create accepts a file path for the ticket description (contents inlined)
